title: Data Structures and Data Types Quiz

questions.text: Which statement best matches Data Structures and Data Types?

options.text: It focuses on the key ideas in data structures and data types.
It is only about hardware repair.
It is unrelated to GCSE Computer Science.
It is only about office software.

questions.explanation: This topic covers specification content linked to data structures and data types.

questions.text: Why should students revise data structures and data types?

options.text: Because it is part of the WJEC legacy specification.
Because it is not examined at all.
Because it only appears at A level.
Because it replaces mathematics.

questions.explanation: This content comes from the WJEC GCSE Computer Science legacy specification.

questions.text: What is the best revision approach for data structures and data types?

options.text: Learn definitions, apply ideas and practise exam questions.
Only memorise one keyword.
Ignore examples and explanations.
Revise it once at the end only.

questions.explanation: Effective revision combines knowledge, understanding and practice.

questions.text: What should learners be able to do in data structures and data types?

options.text: Explain ideas clearly and apply them in context.
Only copy notes word for word.
Avoid using technical terms.
Skip all practical examples.

questions.explanation: The specification expects both understanding and application.

questions.text: What is most useful when revising data structures and data types?

options.text: Accurate technical vocabulary and worked examples.
Random guessing
Learning unrelated facts
Ignoring key terms

questions.explanation: Technical vocabulary and examples help learners succeed in exam questions.
